paper: ParetOrch: Cost-Quality Pareto Optimization for Multi-Agent Orchestration

mismatches.description: Discussion daily/annual savings estimates are arithmetically wrong

locations.discussion: Section 6.1, paragraph 1: 'approximately $160/day or $58K/year in reduced API spend'

expected_values.derivation: Always-debate total cost = $3.20 per 84 tasks. Per task = $0.03810. 10,000 tasks/day = $381. ParetOrch total cost = $1.49 per 84 tasks. Per task = $0.01774. 10,000 tasks/day = $177. Savings = $381 - $177 = $204/day. Annual = $204 * 365 = $74,460.

mismatches.fix: Replace '$160/day or $58K/year' with '$204/day or $74K/year' in Discussion Section 6.1. Alternatively, if the $160 figure comes from a different baseline assumption (e.g., not 100% debate), state the assumption explicitly.

mismatches.description: Cost CI width claim in Results does not match Table 1 CIs

locations.results_table1: Table 1 CIs range from +/-$0.01 to +/-$0.02 (CI width = $0.02-$0.04)

expected_values.derivation: Table 1 shows ParetOrch cost CI = +/-$0.01, meaning CI width = $0.02. Always-Flat CI = +/-$0.01 (width $0.02). Random CI = +/-$0.02 (width $0.04). Mean CI width across all five policies = ($0.02 + $0.04 + $0.02 + $0.02 + $0.02) / 5 = $0.024. None of these yield $0.0014.
expected_values.possible_explanation: The $0.0014 may refer to per-task cost CI (total CI / 84), but this is not stated and would still not match: $0.02 / 84 = $0.000238.

mismatches.fix: Recompute the mean CI width for cost from Table 1 data. If $0.0014 refers to per-task CI, state units explicitly. Most likely the correct value is ~$0.024 for total cost CI width or ~$0.0003 for per-task.

mismatches.description: Discussion cites '89% of cost variance' for multiplicative model, but this number appears nowhere in Methods or Results

locations.discussion: Section 6.4: 'Our multiplicative approximation captures 89% of cost variance'
locations.methods: Section 3.2 discusses the multiplicative cost model but cites overhead ratios (2.10+/-0.08, 2.80+/-0.11) and Kruskal-Wallis p=0.34, not R-squared or variance explained
locations.results: No cost variance analysis appears in any results table

expected_values.derivation: No derivation possible -- the 89% figure has no source in the paper. It may come from an R-squared of the multiplicative model fit, but this was never reported.
expected_values.correct_value: Unknown -- needs to be computed and reported in Methods or Results, or removed from Discussion

mismatches.fix: Either (a) compute and report R-squared of the multiplicative cost model in Section 3.2 or Section 5.5, then reference that result in Discussion, or (b) remove the 89% claim and replace with qualitative language ('captures most of the cost variance') referencing the Kruskal-Wallis test.

mismatches.description: Always-Flat cost reduction rounds to 64.4% from data but Table 1 says 64.3%

locations.results: Table 1, Always-Flat row, Cost Reduction column: 64.3%

mismatches.fix: Change 64.3% to 64.4% in Table 1, or verify from raw data that the unrounded ratio is slightly different (e.g., costs may have more decimal places than shown).

mismatches.description: DIT Accuracy-Only cost reduction rounds to 9.7% from data but Table 1 says 9.6%

locations.results: Table 1, DIT Accuracy-Only row, Cost Reduction column: 9.6%

mismatches.fix: Change 9.6% to 9.7% in Table 1, or verify from raw data that underlying costs differ slightly from the rounded values shown.

warnings.description: The 2,016 run count may double-count topology evaluations that are also routing evaluations

locations.experiments: Section 4.4: '756 topology evaluation runs, plus 5 policies x 84 x 3 = 1,260 routing evaluation runs, for a total of 2,016 runs'

warnings.detail: Always-Flat uses the flat column of the 756 topology runs. Always-Debate uses the debate column. These runs appear in both the 756 and the 1,260 counts. If we subtract the 3 always-X policies (3 * 84 * 3 = 756 duplicate runs), the true unique run count is 1,260. However, the paper may intentionally count routing evaluations separately from topology evaluations. A reviewer may question this.
warnings.fix: Either (a) clarify that 2,016 counts evaluations (not unique executions) or (b) recompute: 756 unique topology executions + 2 novel baselines (Random, DIT-Accuracy-Only, Majority-Class) * 84 * 3 = 504 routing decisions on top = 1,260 unique. ParetOrch adds another 84 * 3 = 252 routing evaluations using existing topology data. Total unique executions = 756.

warnings.description: Introduction claims hierarchy dominates above DIT 0.55 and debate above 0.70, but Results only shows tier-level data, not per-DIT-magnitude data

locations.introduction: Paragraph 4: 'Above 0.55, hierarchical dominates. Above 0.70, debate justifies its premium.'
locations.methods: Section 3.3: 'at ||d|| > 0.55, hierarchical outperforms flat by 13.5 points; at ||d|| > 0.70, debate outperforms flat by 25.5 points'

warnings.detail: These thresholds (0.55, 0.70) are plausible inferences from the tier-level data (medium mean DIT = 0.72, hard mean DIT = 1.10) but are not directly verified by Results tables or figures. No results table shows performance binned by DIT magnitude rather than difficulty tier.
warnings.fix: Add a figure or table showing topology accuracy as a function of DIT magnitude (continuous), or soften the 0.55/0.70 claims to reference the difficulty tiers they map to.

warnings.description: Majority-Class baseline is defined in Experiments but absent from Results Table 1

locations.experiments: Section 4.3: 'Majority-Class. Always selects the topology that achieves the highest training-set accuracy across all tasks.'
locations.results: Table 1 contains 5 rows: Always-Flat, Random, DIT Accuracy-Only, Always-Debate, ParetOrch. No Majority-Class row.

warnings.detail: The Experiments section defines 5 baselines. Table 1 shows only 4 baselines + ParetOrch. The Majority-Class baseline is never mentioned again after being defined. If it was run, its results should appear. If it was dropped, remove it from the baselines list.
warnings.fix: Either add Majority-Class results to Table 1 or remove it from the baselines in Section 4.3. If Majority-Class is identical to Always-Debate (if debate has highest training accuracy), state this explicitly.

verified_consistent: 84 tasks count: consistent across abstract, introduction, experiments, results, conclusion
2,016 run count: consistent across abstract, introduction, experiments, conclusion (with double-counting caveat)
53.4% cost reduction: consistent across abstract, introduction, results Table 1, results Table 3, conclusion
95.6% quality retention: consistent across abstract, introduction, results Table 1, conclusion
$0.023 cost per correct answer: consistent between introduction and results
$0.047 always-debate cost per correct answer: consistent between introduction and results
DIT crossover ~0.35: consistent across abstract, introduction, methods, discussion, conclusion
Cost multipliers (1.0x, 2.1x, 2.8x): consistent across introduction, methods, experiments
Difficulty tiers (17+19+24+24=84): consistent in experiments
Table 1 quality retention ratios: all verified against accuracy/81.3%
Table 1 cost/correct: all verified against cost/(accuracy*84)
Table 1 cost reduction: all verified against 1-cost/3.20 (within rounding)
Table 2 difficulty gaps: all verified (1.8pp, 6.1pp, 17.6pp, 25.5pp)
Table 2 hierarchical efficiency: (75.3-61.8)/(79.4-61.8) = 0.767 ~ 0.77 verified
Table 3 tau=0.75 row: matches Table 1 ParetOrch row
Seeds (42, 137, 2024): consistent in experiments and results
p=0.41 on trivial debate-flat gap: consistent between introduction and results
12 task categories: count verified in experiments
Methods tau=0.65 and tau=0.85 values: match Results Table 3
Ablation DIT removal (+19.3% cost): consistent between abstract and results Table 4
Introduction 48.4% cost savings vs DIT acc-only: verified as 1-1.49/2.89 = 48.4%
Introduction 31.3% savings vs random: verified as 1-1.49/2.17 = 31.3%
Per-seed cost reduction values in Section 5.5: plausible and internally consistent (average ~53.4%)
DIT mean magnitudes by tier: 0.07, 0.34, 0.72, 1.10 -- monotonically increasing, consistent

summary.description: One high-severity arithmetic error in Discussion Section 6.1 (daily/annual savings off by ~$44/day or ~$16K/year). One medium-severity ungrounded statistic (89% cost variance). One medium-severity CI width mismatch. Two low-severity rounding discrepancies. Three structural warnings (run double-counting, missing baseline in results, unstated DIT thresholds).
summary.recommendation: Fix M1 (wrong dollar amounts) and M3 (ungrounded 89% claim) before submission. Investigate M2 (CI width). Address W3 (missing Majority-Class baseline) for completeness.
